name: Vescari Estate - PC Quarters
summary: Personal quarters for a Giovanni PC within the Vescari family estate in Scottsdale—a small second-floor bedroom with access to the estate library and scheduled use of ritual rooms.
description:
The Vescari Estate rises from the desert like a transplanted piece of old Italy, its three-story main house surrounded by a ten-foot brick wall that encloses grounds spanning several acres. From the main gate, looking toward the house, a grove of mature olive and cypress trees clusters off to the right near the back of the property, their dark silhouettes swaying against the moonlit sky. A separate garage building, large enough for four vehicles, sits to the left, its doors always closed, always watched. The main house itself is a study in controlled opulence: dark wood paneling, stone floors that echo with every step, and ancestral portraits lining the corridors—generations of Vescari faces watching with eyes that seem to follow movement.

Your haven is a small bedroom on the second floor, tucked away in the west wing where the family's younger members and guests are housed. The room is perhaps twelve by fourteen feet, its single window covered with heavy velvet curtains that block all sunlight. The walls are paneled in dark mahogany, and the floor is polished stone—cool even in the Phoenix heat. A simple bed occupies one corner, its frame carved with subtle ancestral motifs that seem to shift in the candlelight. A small writing desk sits against the opposite wall, its surface scarred from decades of use, holding a leather-bound journal and a fountain pen. The only other furniture is a wardrobe and a reading chair upholstered in faded burgundy velvet. The air carries the scent of old wood, beeswax, and something else—something dry and papery, like ancient books and forgotten crypts.

Access to the library on the first floor is yours by right—a privilege of clan membership. The library occupies what was once a grand ballroom, its high ceilings now lined with floor-to-ceiling bookshelves containing volumes in Italian, Latin, and English. The collection spans centuries: treatises on necromancy, family genealogies, business ledgers, and works on death, mourning, and the afterlife. Reading tables are arranged in the center, each lit by its own candelabra. The psychological weight of this space isn't in its opulence, but in its silence—the knowledge that every book here contains secrets, that every page has been read by ancestors who now rest in the family crypt, and that the dead themselves might be watching from the shadows between the shelves.

Ritual rooms require scheduling—formal requests made to the estate's administrator, approval granted based on family hierarchy and the nature of the work. These chambers are in the basement, accessible only through locked doors, their locations known but their contents hidden. You've seen one once, during a guided tour: stone walls carved with symbols that hurt to look at directly, an altar of black marble, and the sense that the very air was thin, stretched, as if something was trying to push through from the other side. The scheduled access means you must plan your necromantic work in advance, but it also means you're never alone down there—the family always knows who's using the ritual spaces and when.

But the most unsettling thing about living in this estate isn't the restrictions or the formality. It's the knowledge that you're never truly alone. The portraits watch. The family's ghouls move through the halls like ghosts. And sometimes, in the deepest hours of the night, you hear voices—whispers in Italian that stop when you listen, footsteps in empty corridors, the sound of someone breathing just outside your door. The Vescari don't just commune with the dead. They live among them. And in this house, the line between the living and the dead is thinner than anywhere else in Phoenix.
notes: Potential haven if PC is of that Clan. Part of the larger Vescari family estate in Scottsdale (Camelback Mountain area). The estate serves as the Giovanni family stronghold in Phoenix, housing multiple family members, ghouls, and extensive business operations. This entry focuses specifically on the PC's personal quarters and access privileges. The full estate will be detailed in a separate, comprehensive location entry comparable to the Hawthorne Estate. PC access includes: personal bedroom (2nd floor, west wing), library access (1st floor, unrestricted), and scheduled ritual room access (basement, requires approval). The estate's security, full layout, and other facilities will be documented in the main estate entry.
status_notes: Operational as part of the Vescari family estate. PC quarters maintained according to family standards. Access privileges subject to family hierarchy and good standing within the clan.
address: Vescari Estate - exact address classified (family property)
owner_notes: Owned and controlled by the Vescari family, a branch of the Giovanni clan. The estate serves as the Giovanni stronghold in Phoenix. PC has residence rights as a clan member but does not own the property. Access and privileges are granted based on family hierarchy and standing within the clan.
access_notes: Access strictly controlled by family hierarchy. PC has access to: personal bedroom (2nd floor), library (1st floor, unrestricted), and ritual rooms (basement, scheduled access only). Other areas of the estate require explicit permission. The estate is guarded by family ghouls and protected by the ten-foot brick wall surrounding the property. Electronic surveillance and traditional security measures protect the perimeter. Family members and trusted ghouls have full access; visitors require invitation and escort.
security_notes: Maximum security estate. Ten-foot brick wall surrounds entire property. Main house has reinforced doors and windows. Electronic surveillance system monitors perimeter and key interior areas. Family ghouls serve as guards, patrolling grounds and monitoring access. Ritual warding protects against supernatural intrusion. PC's bedroom window covered with heavy curtains, completely sealed against sunlight. Estate garage secured separately. The grove of trees provides additional cover and concealment. All access points monitored and controlled. Family hierarchy determines security clearances for different areas of the estate.
utility_notes: Blood storage: Estate maintains blood storage facilities (location and access subject to family hierarchy). Library: Extensive first-floor library with centuries of collected volumes on necromancy, family history, business records, and esoteric knowledge. PC has unrestricted access during appropriate hours. Communications: Estate has phone lines, and family maintains communication networks. No computers in PC quarters—family prefers traditional methods. Other utilities (medical, workshop, armory, hidden caches) may exist elsewhere in the estate but are not part of PC's direct access.
social_features: PC quarters are part of a larger family estate that serves as social hub for Giovanni clan in Phoenix. The library functions as informal gathering space for family members. Formal family meetings and business functions occur in other areas of the estate (not part of PC's direct access). The estate's opulent setting and Italian aesthetic create an atmosphere of old-world sophistication and family tradition. Capacity for social functions is extensive (full estate), but PC's personal space is limited to small bedroom. Social standing within the family determines access to various estate facilities and events.
ritual_space: Ritual rooms located in basement, accessible through scheduled approval process. These chambers are designed for necromantic work, with stone walls carved with symbols, black marble altars, and spaces that feel thin, stretched, as if something is trying to push through from the other side. PC must schedule access in advance through estate administrator. The ritual spaces are part of the larger estate's necromantic infrastructure.
magical_protection: Ritual warding protects the estate against supernatural intrusion. The family's necromantic practices create layers of protection that extend beyond the physical. The estate's very structure seems to be aware, with ancestral portraits that watch and the sense that the dead are present throughout the house. The grove of trees may serve additional protective purposes known only to the family elders.
cursed_blessed: The estate is both blessing and burden. Living here provides access to resources, knowledge, and protection, but it also means constant surveillance, family hierarchy, and the ever-present awareness that you're never truly alone. The dead are always watching, always present, and the line between the living and the dead is thinner here than anywhere else in Phoenix.
relationship_notes: PC quarters are part of the larger Vescari Estate. This entry represents the PC's personal haven space (bedroom, library access, ritual room access) within the comprehensive estate location. The full estate will be documented separately as a major location comparable to the Hawthorne Estate, including all floors, the garage, grounds, grove, and full facilities.
